submission.questionnaireText:
Justin Morin

1. Do you gain weight easily?
(1) I do not experience this
2. [IF SO] Where do you typically gain weight on your body?
Uniformly
3. Do you feel "puffy," i.e your fat tends to be soft and feel like water retention?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
4. Do you struggle with unintended weight loss?
(1) I do not experience this
5. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you accumulate cellulite?
Not Applicable
6. Do you struggle with low appetite?
(1) I do not experience this
7. Do you tend to wake up when sleeping?
(1) I do not experience this
8. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, is it: within the first few hours of falling asleep OR later on in the night OR both?
I do not wake up when sleeping
9. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, is it to: urinate OR eat something OR drink something OR seemingly no reason?
I do not wake up when sleeping
10. If you tend to wake up when sleeping, do you find that you are hot or sweaty upon waking?
I do not wake up when sleeping
11. Do you NOT dream?
(1) I do not experience this
12. Do you experience nightmares or sleep paralysis?
(1) I do not experience this
13. Do you have trouble falling asleep?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
14. Do you feel like you DON'T get a restful sleep?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
15. Do you frequently feel cold, especially at the extremities (fingertips, toes, and nose)?
(1) I do not experience this
16. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you frequently have painful menstrual cramps?
Not Applicable
17. [WOMEN ONLY] Do you frequently have irregular menstruation or suffer from premenstrual syndrome?
Not Applicable
18. Do you suffer from chronic congestion without mucus?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
19. Do you frequently experience mucus buildup in the throat or nose?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
20. [IF SO] Is it exacerbated after eating?
No
21. Do you suffer from dental problems such as bad breath, cavities, and gum inflammation?
(1) I do not experience this
22. Do you often find that you are irritable or easily agitated?
(1) I do not experience this
23. Do you suffer from "brain fog," low energy or feel like you do not have mental clarity?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
24. Do you tend to have an "afternoon crash" after eating lunch?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
25. Do you often feel "hyper aware," jittery, or restless?
(1) I do not experience this
26. Do you notice the sensation of your heart pounding or difficulty breathing?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
27. Have you had a loss of pleasure in activities you normally enjoy?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
28. Have you noticed a lack of sexual interest or desire?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
29. [MEN ONLY] Have you lost morning erections?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
30. Do you have skin issues such as dry skin, eczema, or acne?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
31. Can you trace a worsening of your symptoms back to a traumatic event?
No
32. Do you have specific food cravings? If so, please list the foods you crave.
No
33. Do you have a history of mental health issues? If so, please list them.
No
34. Do you experience constipation?
(1) I do not experience this
35. Do you experience nausea?
(1) I do not experience this
36. Do you experience bloating (subjective feeling)?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
37. Do you experience distention (visible expansion of the abdomen)?
(3) This is a fairly bothersome issue for me
38. Do you experience diarrhea?
(1) I do not experience this
39. Do you experience belching?
(1) I do not experience this
40. Do you experience flatulence?
(1) I do not experience this
41. Do you experience pain in your upper abdomen?
(1) I do not experience this
42. Do you experience pain in your right upper quadrant?
(1) I do not experience this
43. Do you experience pain in your lower abdomen?
(1) I do not experience this
44. Are you aware of any specific foods that trigger your symptoms? If so, please list them.
No
45. Do you experience dry stools?
(1) I do not experience this
46. Do you experience acid reflux?
(1) I do not experience this
47. Do your symptoms worsen IMMEDIATELY after eating?
(2) This is a somewhat bothersome issue for me
48. Do your symptoms worsen 1-3 hours after eating?
(1) I do not experience this
49. Do symptoms alleviate after a bowel movement?
No
50. Have you tried anything in the past to alleviate your symptoms? These can be dietary changes, supplements, prescriptions or anything else. If so, please list them, and how helpful (or unhelpful) they were for you. Please be as specific as possible, including specific foods and brands and dosages of supplements, as well as what symptoms they affected.
Yes
I think a primary thing that caused my issues overtime was that i did intermittent fasting for like 5+ years almost daily.
I even tried things like keto and carnivore for shorter periods of time.
I later on learned that's not ideal, i not have something for breakfast daily and include carbs in every meal.
i assume from that some of issues are caused from a thyroid issue because of my prolonged fasting.
I also assume i have some kind of bacterial or fungal overgrowth.
Main issues are fatigue, brain fog, tension/sinus headaches, consistent nasal congestion with and without mucus. Acute allergies to pet dander and smoke etc..
I notice i bloat very frequently and quickly after eating most things.
typically all of my symptoms of congestion, headache, fatigue are all worse in the first couple hours of the day
51. Are you currently taking any supplements? If so, please list them and be as specific as possible, including the brand and dosage of any supplement. Please mention how helpful (or unhelpful) you perceive them to be.
Yes
Lactoferrin - 500mg
Vitamin D3-K2 combo - 10kUI D3, 100mcg K2
Aspirin - 650mg
B1 - 1000mg
Magnesium L Threonate - 1000mg
Zinc - 15mg + copper 2mg
Pregnenolone - 200mg
Methylene Blue - 5mg
Mastic Gum - 1 - 2 pieces
Baking soda - half teaspoon
5g of Glutamine + Creatine Combo
52. Are you currently taking any medications? If so, please list them and be as specific as possible, including the brand and dosage of any medication.
Yes
Allegra - D 12hr
usually half pill in the morning, most mornings
full pill or more if about to go to house with pets
53. Are you currently diagnosed with any medical conditions? If so, please list them.
No
54. Please describe your diet. What foods do you typically eat, and in what amounts? Are these foods prepared by you, or are they pre-packaged, or from a restaurant? At what times do you typically eat?
Typical Breakfast

3 eggs

goat cheese

Glass of OJ - usually mix collagen in there

Blueberries

Black coffee usually 1hr 30 after waking - mixed w Methylene Blue and / or collagen

Typical Lunch

Turkey or Pastrami and Cheese sandwich

Deli meat from whole foods

Organic White Bread from whole foods

Avocado Oil Mayo from whole foods

Typical Dinner

Chicken Rice Bowl from Chipotle
or

Homemade cheese or chicken and cheese quesadilla - whole foods ingredients
or

Homemade burger bowl or tacos - whole foods ingredients

buffalo chicken wrap from Diner

on some weekends will have pizza or ice cream sometimes

if in a rush and didn't pack lunch will Dunkin donuts egg cheese turkey sauce wrap or English muffin

will snack on fruit a lot of times

55. Please describe your lifestyle. Do you get outside often? Do you exercise? How stressful is your day-to-day life due to work or other factors?
i work 50+ hours in an office job weekly.

Now that its nicer out i do get outside most days after work for a bit and on the weekends. I do a lot of grounding and stretching outside, walking.

In the last 6-9 months since I've had more working hours + less energy, more fatigue on a daily basis i haven't been in gym lifting or aerobic exercise like i used to for many years prior.

56. Please list any other information you think would help us help you.
25yrs old

6'4 190-200lbs

Main issues

consistent congestion with and without mucus

acute allergies to pet dander

consistent fatigue, tensions headaches above eyes, foggy mental clarity (especially in the first few hours of the day, typically clears up later on and i am functional enough after that but not optimal)

bloat very easily and consistently, to most foods nothing specific it seems

Main suspects

thyroid issue do to fasting for many years

bacterial or fungal overgrowth
submission.takehomeText:
What are your four resting heart beats per minute? Please separate values with commas. [example: 78, 81, 75, 80]
89, 88, 91, 86

How many times did you urinate each day for four days? Please separate values with commas. [example: 6, 6, 8, 7]
7,6,8,7

Does your urine tend to be foamy?
(1) I do not experience this
How many times did you defecate per day for four days? Please separate values with commas. [example: 3, 1, 0, 2]
2,2,2,2

What Types on the Bristol Stool Scale did each of your bowel movements fall under? Please separate values with commas. [example: Type 2, Type 2, Type 4, Type 5, Type 7, Type 1, Type 6]
Type 5, Type 5, Type 4, Type 5, Type 4

What were both of your temperature measurements each morning? Please separate values with commas. [example:
98.2, 98.4]

96.4, 97.2

[WOMEN ONLY] What date did your last period begin? [MEN: SKIP QUESTION]
No answer given.

[WOMEN ONLY] What date did you take your first temperature measurement? [MEN: SKIP QUESTION]
No answer given.

Did your tongue have a white coating each of the four mornings? Please separate values with commas. [example: Yes, Yes, No, Yes]
Yes, Yes, Yes, Yes
submission.advisorNotesText:
Clinician: Shannon Sirois
6:41 PM
Justin is a 25 y.o. M that has recently been working a lot more hours and starting his mornings a bit earlier than he used to. He thinks this change, increased screen time, increased work stress combined with doing intermittent fasting over the past five years may have contributed to his current state.

Worst sx’s: bloating. He feels like not long after he eats and also when he wakes up in the morning he notices his stomach is bloated and sticking out more than it should.

Seasonal allergies: he wakes up every morning congested and full of mucus, and often has acute attacks of allergies where he gets very congested, pet dander is the worst

Fatigue: very tired when he gets up in the morning, takes a while to get going. Feels better mid morning, but tired again in the afternoon.

The only other thing of note was black mold exposure over 6 months ago in an apartment, but they moved since then. May be some of the morning fatigue. B1

He has tried every “Peaty” thing imaginable and still has the gut issues. He’s taking 250mg of lactoferrin and Seed probiotic, and no change. I think he’s a good candidate for a few rounds of minocycline.

25mg 2 x per day for 3 days, then 5 day break. Repeats if necessary

Continue with Seed probiotic AFTER minocycline

K2 Mk4 (he was using Mk7) from Health Natura: 2 drops per day

L Glutamine: he was only doing 5 grams, he’s going to start doing 5 g 3x per day

Thyroid: He just began taking an NDT a few weeks ago. I want to give this a chance to start working. If he doesn’t feel a difference in the next few weeks, then we can talk about testing. Edit: he messaged me, he’s taking Evolved Elements 200mg capsules at 4-5 capsules per day for dosage.

For mold detox in case this is still a problem (may be why his allergies are really bad this year): Shilajit-does anyone have a source for this?? the one I was using is no longer available.

He’s starting on berberine (he already had this at home): already instructed him via messenger

 Triphala: for mobilizing and binding in the digestive tract: 2 capsules in the morning
https://www.amazon.com/ORGANIC-INDIA-Triphala-Digestive-Support/dp/B003PGCUKO/ref=asc_df_B003PGCUKO/?tag=hyprod-20&linkCode=df0&hvadid=693575525460&hvpos=&hvnetw=g&hvrand=14457592425569198770&hvpone=&hvptwo=&hvqmt=&hvdev=c&hvdvcmdl=&hvlocint=&hvlocphy=9002577&hvtargid=pla-659450281891&psc=1&mcid=b9572fa9f84131a99160965044197d72&gad_source=1&gclid=CjwKCAjwouexBhAuEiwAtW_Zx547bd5MsK5yBh3KUevJWoEk6orownkiU80QXBFbttFOOZyV3OYPRBoCufgQAvD_BwE

His diet: He knows what he needs to do here, he does indulge in eating out quite a bit! We talked about lessening that while his gut is healing.

Justin’s Take on his health:
I think a primary thing that caused my issues overtime was that i did intermittent fasting for like 5+ years almost daily.
I even tried things like keto and carnivore for shorter periods of time.
I later on learned that's not ideal, i not have something for breakfast daily and include carbs in every meal.
i assume from that some of issues are caused from a thyroid issue because of my prolonged fasting.
I also assume i have some kind of bacterial or fungal overgrowth.
Main issues are fatigue, brain fog, tension/sinus headaches, consistent nasal congestion with and without mucus. Acute allergies to pet dander and smoke etc..
I notice i bloat very frequently and quickly after eating most things.

typically all of my symptoms of congestion, headache, fatigue are all worse in the first couple hours of the day
Supplements he’s taking:

Lactoferrin - 500mg

Vitamin D3-K2 combo - 10kUI D3, 100mcg K2

Aspirin - 650mg

B1 - 1000mg

Magnesium L Threonate - 1000mg

Zinc - 15mg + copper 2mg

Pregnenolone - 200mg

Methylene Blue - 5mg

Mastic Gum - 1 - 2 pieces

Baking soda - half teaspoon

5g of Glutamine + Creatine Combo

Current Diet:

Typical Breakfast

3 eggs

goat cheese

Glass of OJ - usually mix collagen in there

Blueberries

Black coffee usually 1hr 30 after waking - mixed w Methylene Blue and / or collagen

Typical Lunch

Turkey or Pastrami and Cheese sandwich

Deli meat from whole foods

Organic White Bread from whole foods

Avocado Oil Mayo from whole foods

Typical Dinner

Chicken Rice Bowl from Chipotle
or

Homemade cheese or chicken and cheese quesadilla - whole foods ingredients
or

Homemade burger bowl or tacos - whole foods ingredients

buffalo chicken wrap from Diner

on some weekends will have pizza or ice cream sometimes

if in a rush and didn't pack lunch will Dunkin donuts egg cheese turkey sauce wrap or English muffin

will snack on fruit a lot of times


---

Chart Note
Clinician: Elie Klein
2:51 PM
For the nasal congestion have him use
1. Xclear nasal spray before bed, upon rising and as needed
2. Wild indigo tincture (follow instructions on the bottle)

an alternate nasal spray is sinus Orega

The effect of Minocycline will show us if this is a GI fungal or bacterial issue. If it fails, we may need to offer plan B options.

Can we order a candida stool test?

Diet-wise, He could use an additional serving of fruit per meal and and I believe increased sodium (bicarb) / potassium intake will support decrease in nasal congestion as well:

https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/Airway-secretion-electrolytes%3A-reflection-of-water-Cavaliere-Masieri/4ac7c0bb04fcf7401c3bda534d2d7044edb78856

Show Less
May 9

Chart Note
Clinician: Collin Dees
Edit  
4:01 PM
Niacinamide for antifungal activity

Oregano oil for antifungal and antibacterial activity, I like the Berberine suggestion as well. I believe S. boulardii has some effects on mold and fungus? Could be beneficial to try.

Based on temps and other symptoms, NDT is 100% indicated.
